content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified all four amendments against the codebase and actual br 0.1.35 CLI behavior. Three are clear blockers for `milestone export-beads`: br show returns an array so exec_json::<BeadDetail> fails; br rejects --label (requires --labels/-l) so every create_bead call fails; closed/deferred beads turn re-exports into hard errors. The fourth identifies a real correctness gap where bundle validation allows duplicate titles with explicit bead_ids but the matcher keys only on title, silently breaking idempotency.

## Votes

- `fr-3-e76b2f84`: Accept
  Verified: `br show <id> --json` returns a one-element array (confirmed by running against this repo's .beads/). BeadDetail (src/adapters/br_models.rs:268) is a plain struct, not wrapped. exec_json::<BeadDetail> at service.rs:1786, 1801, 2015 will serde-fail with BrParseError on real output. Other call sites (cli/project.rs:1132) correctly use BrShowResponse untagged enum (Single/Many), demonstrating the author knew about the array form. Tests mock an object payload (service.rs:5278) rather than an array, so the bug hides in tests. Real blocker for milestone export-beads.
- `fr-3-6bfeb81e`: Accept
  Verified at service.rs:1900-1908 and 1968-1981. summary_matches_materialized_bead_except_role gates on `matches!(summary.status, Open | InProgress)`, so closed/deferred title matches become ineligible, and find_existing_materialized_bead then returns a hard error with 'incompatible type or non-active status'. Re-running export-beads after normal workflow (some work completed or deferred) fails instead of skipping those proposals and exporting newly added work. This breaks the command's expected incremental/idempotent semantics; correct behavior is to treat the proposal as already satisfied by the closed/deferred bead.
- `fr-3-8f748c9e`: Accept
  Verified at br_process.rs:1342-1344 (kv("label", ...) emits --label=<v>) and build_args at 312-314. Ran `br create --label=alpha --dry-run` against br 0.1.35: 'error: unexpected argument --label found; tip: a similar argument exists: --labels'. `br create --help` confirms only -l/--labels (comma-separated). materialized_bead_labels always injects milestone:<id>, so every materialize_beads create_bead call fails at the parser — no beads created, no deps wired, command never reaches journal emit. Test at br_process.rs:2227-2248 asserts the wrong shape because it never invokes br. Hard blocker.
- `fr-3-f08e0ba4`: Accept
  Verified at bundle.rs:224-234: the 'duplicates non-explicit proposal' check only fires when `!has_explicit_id`, so two proposals with identical titles AND explicit bead_ids pass validation. find_existing_materialized_bead (service.rs:1940-1982) keys existing_by_title solely on normalized title and bails at 1958-1966 when eligible_indices.len() > 1. On re-export, the two previously-created beads (same title, same milestone:<id> label, distinct br IDs) trigger the 'multiple milestone-scoped beads already match' error with no disambiguation path. Real correctness gap — either validation must forbid duplicate titles unconditionally, or the matcher must consult the canonical proposal_id (via label/comment/description marker).
